title: The harpies GNR and Seeles Personal Ark
display_title: The harpies GNR and Seeles Personal Ark
wikitext:
[]: The harpies, GNR and Seele's "Personal Ark"             Quote Post by Ragehammer » Sat Oct 01, 2011 12:46 am MASSIVE EOE SPOILERS (unsure if that is needed) Hello guys, this is my first thread here, but I have read these forums for a while, and whilst not entirely a noob to evangelion; this particular issue has never quite made much sense to me. Feel free to discuss it as well, as I am sure there will be many different interpretations to it (as always, somedays I wish Anno would just explain, but that would ruin the fun O.o) In EoE, the main groups (Gendo and Seele) have very different outcomes in causing instrumentality, and I am aware that this is a lower level of canon, but we possibly get a fairly good look at Seele's "ideal" outcome in NGE2, whereby only they merge into the Eva, and then destroy the lance; therefore becoming a permanent god. There is some dialogue at the start of EoE, about how they do not need to abandon their human forms to use Eva asa "personal ark" that supports this. However, what I cannot for the life of me figure out, is what the mass production Evas were actually for. Seele (Keel in particular) seemed pretty happy with the actual instrumentality that occured, but were they trying to perform the scenario described in NGE2? Lillith was supposedly unusuable to them with the lance gone (at least thats the reason that is chucked around, I cannot find the source in series, and would be grareful if someone knew). Where the harpies actually neccesary if instrumentality was simply to be done with lillith/adam, or were they only used because unit 01 was acting as a substitute for lillith? Additionally, as unit 01 is part of lillith, why did it need to be first turned into the tree of life (and how on Earth did that work?), before moving into Lillith's main body? Or is this the "supplimentation of humanity" that Seele speaks of? These are my main question, but also, is there any rational process behind why the stabbed themselsves as they did (or any of the other stuff)? Thanks in advance guys, discuss :) Edit: Fixing my derps

[]: Meh. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 01, 2011 2:54 am Ragehammer wrote: Feel free to discuss it as well, as I am sure there will be many different interpretations to it

[]:             Quote Post by Ragehammer » Sat Oct 01, 2011 5:42 am Wow! Thanks a bunch for that! On on the canon thing, my bad; I phrased that poorly. I was referring to the NGE2 Video game parts where "Seele Wins" there.
